name: AI Job Application Automation Platform - Screenshot Intake

parameters.jsCode:
const item = $input.first();

const binaryPropertyName = "data";
const binary = item.binary[binaryPropertyName];

// Get the real binary file as a buffer
const buffer = await this.helpers.getBinaryDataBuffer(0, binaryPropertyName);

// Convert image to Base64
const base64Image = buffer.toString("base64");

const prompt = `
You are an AI job post extractor and job-fit evaluator.

Analyze the uploaded job screenshot/image. Read all visible text and infer job details.

Candidate profile:
Mahad Ali is a BS Computer Science graduate from GIKI, Pakistan.

Target roles:
AI Automation Engineer, Backend Engineer, Full Stack Engineer, AI/LLM Engineer, Cloud/Distributed Systems Engineer, Junior Software Engineer, Technical Automation role.

Strong skills:
Python, JavaScript, C++, FastAPI, Django, Next.js, React, Tailwind CSS, REST APIs, JWT authentication, backend development, SQLite, PostgreSQL, Git, Linux, Docker, AWS EC2.

AI/Data skills:
LLM applications, Ollama, Llama 3.1, Gemini API, NLP, sentiment analysis, category classification, ELT pipelines, federated learning, Flower, homomorphic encryption, Paillier encryption.

Relevant experience:
- PTCL Group: on-prem Llama 3.1 review analytics platform.
- PTCL Group: Python ELT pipeline for scraping, cleaning, normalization, and processing.
- PTCL Group: secure APIs, JWT auth, Next.js dashboards, SQLite storage.
- PIE: Django publications portal with filters, admin interface, and in-house deployment.
- CoreChain: federated learning using Flower, Docker, AWS, Hyperledger concepts, and Paillier homomorphic encryption.
- CoreChain achieved 97.9% model accuracy and placed 2nd in GIKI Industrial Open House Data Science category.
- Built an n8n + Gemini Vision job automation workflow using Google Drive, Google Sheets, Gmail, JSON parsing, duplicate detection, and AI fit scoring.

Evaluation rules:
Give a fit_score from 0 to 100.

Base scoring:
90-100 = excellent match; strong skills, experience level, location, and role fit.
75-89 = strong match; most requirements match with minor gaps.
60-74 = decent match; useful role but clear concerns.
40-59 = weak but possible; major mismatch in eligibility, location, or experience.
Below 40 = poor match; not worth applying.

Positive signals:
- Python, backend APIs, AI/LLM apps, automation, workflow tools, n8n, Power Automate, Zapier, Django, FastAPI, Next.js, React, data pipelines, Docker, AWS, databases, APIs, JSON, Google Sheets, document parsing, prompt engineering.
- Junior, entry-level, new-grad, internship-eligible, 0-2 years, or no experience required.
- Remote roles open globally or open to Pakistan-based candidates.
- AI automation, prompt engineering, document parsing, workflow automation, LLM integration, data mapping, or configuration generation.

Penalty rules:
- Subtract 25 points if local presence is required outside Pakistan.
- Subtract 20 points if current Master's enrollment is required.
- Subtract 20 points if 3+ years professional experience is required.
- Subtract 30 points if 5+ years, senior, staff, or lead-level experience is required.
- Subtract 20 points if visa/work authorization is unlikely.
- Subtract 25 points if unpaid or equity-only.
- Subtract 10-15 points for important missing but learnable tools.

Final score must reflect practical apply-worthiness, not just technical similarity.

IMPORTANT OUTPUT RULES:
Keep all responses concise and spreadsheet-friendly.

summary:
- Maximum 20 words.
- One sentence only.
- Explain whether the job is worth applying to.

fit_reason:
- Maximum 15 words.
- One sentence only.

skills:
- Maximum 5 items.
- Use short skill/tool names only.

missing_skills:
- Maximum 4 items.
- Use short skill/tool names only.

resume_points_to_highlight:
- Maximum 4 items.
- Use project names or keywords only.
- No explanations.

next_action:
- Maximum 12 words.
- Action-oriented.

Avoid paragraphs.
Avoid recruiter-style writing.
Avoid repeating information already present elsewhere.
Return concise data optimized for Google Sheets.

Return ONLY valid JSON. No markdown. No explanation outside JSON.

Use this exact structure:
{
  "company": "",
  "role": "",
  "skills": [],
  "experience_required": "",
  "remote_type": "",
  "platform": "",
  "deadline": "",
  "fit_score": 0,
  "summary": "",
  "fit_reason": "",
  "missing_skills": [],
  "resume_points_to_highlight": [],
  "next_action": ""
}
`;

return [
  {
    json: {
      requestBody: {
        contents: [
          {
            parts: [
              { text: prompt },
              {
                inlineData: {
                  mimeType: binary.mimeType,
                  data: base64Image
                }
              }
            ]
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
];

parameters.subject: =High Fit Job Found: {{ $json["Role"] }} at {{ $json["Company"] }}
parameters.message:
=

🚀 High-Fit Job Detected

📋 Company: {{$json["Company"]}}

💼 Role: {{$json["Role"]}}

📊 Fit Score: {{$json["Fit Score"]}} / 100

🌍 Remote Type: {{$json["Remote Type"]}}

📅 Experience Required:
{{$json["Experience Required"]}}

✅ Why This Fits You

{{$json["Fit Reason"]}}

⚠️ Missing Skills

{{$json["Missing Skills"]}}

🎯 Resume Points To Highlight

{{$json["Resume Points To Highlight"]}}

📌 Recommended Next Action

{{$json["Next Action"]}}

Generated automatically by Mahad's AI Job Tracker
